item.title: India Unleashes Curbs on Rupee Bets as Intervention Costs Swell
item.description: India has moved to curb speculative bets against the rupee, taking one of its most forceful steps in over a decade as the cost of defending the currency rises.

item.title: Japan’s FX Chief Warns of ‘Bold’ Action After Yen Tops 160
item.description: Japan’s top currency official delivered his strongest warning yet to speculators, saying authorities may need to take bold action in the foreign exchange market if current conditions persist.

item.title: Yen Edges Away From Weakest Since 2024 Amid Intervention Risk
item.description: The yen edged away from its weakest level since July 2024 amid warnings that suggest Japan is prepared for potential intervention to support the currency.

item.title: Japanese Stocks Drop as Iran Conflict Escalates, Oil Price Rises
item.description: Japanese stocks declined after concerns over the escalating Iran war and climbing oil prices hit risk appetite as the conflict enters its fifth week.

item.title: Aluminum Surges 6% After Iran Strikes Plants in UAE, Bahrain
item.description: Aluminum climbed around 6% after Iran attacked two production sites in the Middle East, threatening to deepen supply disruptions in a region that accounts for a significant portion of global output.

item.title: JX Metals Plans to Expand Investment as Chip Demand Surges
item.description: JX Advanced Metals Corp. is planning to invest more in materials for chips and information technology as demand for semiconductors surges.

item.title: Foreigners Dump Record $12 Billion India Stocks in March on War
item.description: Foreign investors are fleeing Indian equities at a record clip as a global retreat from riskier assets and concerns over surging energy costs overshadow the nation’s long-term growth story.

item.title: ECB Must Anchor Inflation Expectations, Villeroy Tells La Stampa
item.description: The European Central Bank is prepared to act to rein in inflation expectations, though betting on dates for potential interest-rate hikes is premature, Governing Council member Francois Villeroy de Galhau told La Stampa.

item.title: US Plans to Allow Russian Oil Tanker Into Cuba, Easing Crisis
item.description: The Trump administration is planning to let a Russian oil tanker dock in Cuba, alleviating an energy crisis triggered when the US prohibited deliveries to the Communist regime.

item.title: CTG Duty Free Looks to Earnings Catalyst to Snap 39% Rout
item.description: China Tourism Group Duty Free Corp.’s shares may be poised to shift course after a weak run this year, as stabilizing sales and improving demand from its key Hainan business support the outlook, analysts say.

item.title: Gold Steadies as Iran War Enters Fifth Week With No End in Sight
item.description: Gold held its first weekly gain since the war in the Middle East began, as dip-buyers stepped into the market.

item.title: Brent Oil Advances After Houthis Join Iran Conflict
item.description: A Palestinian flag is raised as Houthis rally in solidarity with Iran and Lebanon, in Sanaa, Yemen, on March 27, 2026.

item.title: Oil Gains as Iran War Escalates With Houthi Attacks on Israel
item.description: Oil advanced as Iran-backed Houthi militants in Yemen entered the Middle East war and more US troops arrived in the region, raising fears the widening conflict will cause further chaos for energy markets.

item.title: Iran War’s Gas Supply Shock Pushes Top Consumers Back to Coal
item.description: A deepening conflict in the Persian Gulf has upended oil and gas markets. It may also have provided coal — the dirtiest fossil fuel — with its most significant boost in years.

item.title: Are Australia’s Low Fuel Reserves Cause for Concern?
item.description: Despite being a major energy exporter, Australia relies heavily on imported refined fuel to power transport, agriculture and other parts of its economy, leaving it highly exposed to disruptions in global supply chains.

item.title: Investor Unease Builds Entering War’s Fifth Week
item.description: A record month for crude, stocks in or near correction territory, bonds under pressure and a growing sense that there are few tools to shield markets from an increasingly entrenched Iran war.

item.title: Apollo Pushes to Open a Second Headquarters in Florida or Texas
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc. is looking to open a second US headquarters and is considering basing it in south Florida or Texas, the company said Sunday.

item.title: JPMorgan, Pimco Say Bond Market Is Underestimating Slowdown Risk
item.description: Some of Wall Street’s biggest bond-fund managers say financial markets are underestimating the risk that the US war in Iran will cause a sharp slowdown in an already sputtering economy.

item.title: Philippine Refiner Petron Buys Russian Crude, Eyes More If Iran War Persists
item.description: Petron Corp., the Philippines’ only refiner, has procured 2.48 million barrels of crude oil from Russia as the Southeast Asian nation scours the world for alternative suppliers to support domestic energy needs with the war in Iran raging.

item.title: French Factory Closures Jump 30% on Asia Pressure, US Tariffs
item.description: French factory closures jumped almost 30% last year after rising pressure from Asian competitors and the blow from US tariffs, while the number of new openings also declined.

item.title: Wall Street Touts ‘Grind Lower’ Trades as Iran Weighs on Stocks
item.description: As the Iran war heads into a fifth week, Wall Street bank strategists have been touting trades that would pay off if a stock-market selloff is slow and steady.

item.title: Why Is NASA Spending $100 Billion to Return to the Moon? Depends on Who You Ask
item.description: NASA pulled off the moon landing in 1969 with a clear goal from the outset: to be the first nation to put boots on the lunar surface.

item.title: WTO Members Debate Five-Year Extension of E-Commerce Tariff Ban
item.description: World Trade Organization members are weighing a five-year extension of a global ban on e-commerce tariffs, according to a draft statement circulating Sunday.

item.title: Event Wagers Face $143 Million Insider Problem as War Bets Boom
item.description: Early last Monday, a burst of concentrated trades set off a week in which Wall Street and Washington were abuzz about the possibility that insiders have been using early knowledge of President Donald Trump’s decision making on Iran to lock in big profits.

item.title: Hormuz Tracker: Saudi Crude Heads to Pakistan in Rare Transit
item.description: A small cargo of Saudi crude is heading to Pakistan after crossing the Strait of Hormuz along a route hugging the Iranian coastline. A total of seven vessels were visible leaving the Persian Gulf on Saturday.

item.title: Emerging Markets Rout Lures Contrarians Betting on Rate Cuts
item.description: With emerging markets on the brink of their worst month since 2022, TT International and AllianceBernstein are making a bold wager that this is a good time to buy.

item.title: Apple Pivots Its AI Strategy to App Store, Search-Like Platform Approach
item.description: Also: The company finally kills the Mac Pro.

item.title: Telecom Italia Scraps Plan to Renew Inwit Tower Contract in 2030
item.description: Telecom Italia SpA said it won’t renew a mobile-phone tower agreement with Inwit SpA that would have extended the contract through 2038.

item.title: Syria Eyes $1 Billion Revival for Postal and Mobile Networks
item.description: Syria is seeking foreign investors for a $1 billion drive to revamp its mobile-phone coverage and postal service, as the war-torn country rebuilds after the US lifted sanctions.

item.title: Banks Urge RBI to Relax New Rules as $30 Billion Unwinding Looms
item.description: Lenders in India are urging the central bank to relax tighter rules for foreign-exchange transactions, which came as bets that could exert strain on the rupee climbed to at least $30 billion, according to people familiar with the developments.
